top_categories.workout_context: 7,776ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
89ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Gran Fondo Medellín, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Medellín's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before October.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Medellín. Train it until it feels boring.
Gran Fondo Medellín's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 87 miles.
7,776ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
At mile 60 of 87, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 87 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Low-cadence force work for 7,776ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 87 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Medellín, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Medellín’s 87 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Gran Fondo Medellín with 7,776ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo Medellín requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, TT Threshold, and Over Under to prepare you for 87 miles of mixed terrain in Medellín, Antioquia, Colombia.
